title: pedicularis verticillata subsp. verticillata
intro: Pedicularis verticillata subsp. verticillata, also known as the whorled lousewort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. The herbal preparations of Pedicularis verticillata subsp. verticillata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, tonics, and ointments for medicinal purposes.
